reasoning: The question asks whether Richard Rapport's Elo rating on Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page will be at least 1% higher on March 22, 2026 compared to March 15, 2026. FIDE releases official ratings on the 1st of every month, and the Wikipedia page 'FIDE rankings' is updated to reflect this monthly list. The same March 2026 ratings list will be present on both March 15 and March 22. Therefore, Rapport's extracted Elo rating will be identical on both dates (at approximately 2738), resulting in a 0% change, not >1%. I assign a 5% probability accounting for edge cases such as page vandalism on the specific resolution date.
